Nuova camorra pugliese.description: The Nuova camorra pugliese was a criminal organization based in Apulia, Italy, active from the early 1970s to the mid-1980s. It was founded by Raffaele Cutolo as an offshoot of the Nuova Camorra Organizzata. The group operated mainly in the provinces of Foggia and Taranto. It was eventually absorbed by the Sacra corona unita.

Goto-gumi.description: The Goto-gumi was a Japanese yakuza organization founded by Tadamasa Goto. Originally based in Fujinomiya, Shizuoka Prefecture, it moved to Tokyo in 1991 and became an affiliate of the Yamaguchi-gumi. The gang was involved in financial, real estate, construction, entertainment, and political activities, and was notorious for the assault on filmmaker Juzo Itami. It was dissolved in 2008 after Goto's expulsion from the Yamaguchi-gumi.

Charles Martel Group.description: French far-right anti-Arab militant group active from 1973 to 1987. Formed by veterans of the Organisation armée secrète, it targeted Algerian properties, businesses, and government offices in France. The group claimed responsibility for several bombings, including the 1973 Algerian consulate bombing in Marseille. Its status is inactive, with no members ever arrested.

It emerged after a power struggle and defection to the Jalisco New Generation Cartel. The group is involved in drug trafficking across the Guatemala-Mexico border and has published execution videos. Its founder was killed in 2025.

'Ndrina Faillace.description: The 'Ndrina Faillace is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cassano all'Ionio, Calabria, Italy. It operates in the Sibari Plain and the province of Cosenza, belonging to the Locale of Sibari. The group emerged victorious from a feud with the Abruzzese-Pepe clan of Lauropoli, alongside the Forastefano and Portoraro families. In 2009, assets including land, vehicles, and companies were confiscated from the Faillace family.

Pietraperzia Mafia family.description: The Pietraperzia Mafia family is a crime family of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra based in Pietraperzia, a town in the province of Enna. It has deep historical roots dating back to at least 1900 and reemerged in the 1980s after a period of inactivity. The family is a close ally of the powerful Catania Mafia family and was involved in high-level mafia operations, including preparatory meetings for the 1992 Capaci bombing. It is currently active.

Guadalajara Cartel.description: The Guadalajara Cartel was the first exclusively drug-trafficking criminal organization in Mexico, formed around 1978 by Miguel Ángel Félix Gallardo, Rafael Caro Quintero, and Ernesto Fonseca Carrillo. It controlled nearly all drug trafficking operations in Mexico during the late 1970s and 1980s, trafficking opium, marijuana, and cocaine to the United States. The cartel was dismantled in 1989 following the arrest of its leaders.

Sinaloa Cartel.description: Mexican transnational criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illicit activities. Founded in 1989 by Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada, and others. Based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, it operates across much of Mexico and the US border, and is considered one of the most influential criminal organizations globally. Currently divided into factions led by Iván Archivaldo Guzmán Salazar (Los Chapitos) and Ismael Zambada Sicairos (La Mayiza).

edges.evidence_quote: El Cártel de Tijuana ha mantenido cierta rivalidad con dos de las organizaciones de tráfico de drogas ilícitas en México, estos son el Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Rivalry with assassination attempts on leaders.
edges.evidence_quote: En la rivalidad que han mantenido el Cártel de Tijuana y el Cártel de Sinaloa , destacan los intentos de homicidio contra los capos de ambas organizaciones

descriptions: Shootout at Guadalajara airport between the two cartels.
edges.evidence_quote: el 24 de mayo de 1993, el Cártel de Tijuana sostiene un enfrentamiento en el estacionamiento del Aeropuerto Internacional de Guadalajara contra el Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Listed as enemy in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Tijuana Cartel charges Sinaloa Cartel a toll for trafficking in its territory, indicating a cooperative arrangement.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tijuana cartel charges a toll ("piso") on the Sinaloa cartel for trafficking drugs in their territory

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel faction attempted to form an alliance with Tijuana Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: emissaries from the Los Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel had been seen in Tijuana attempting to form an alliance with the now resurgent Tijuana Cartel

descriptions: Tijuana Cartel formed a truce and sought merger with Sinaloa Cartel in 2011.
edges.evidence_quote: captured cartel lieutenant Francisco Sillas Rocha had confessed that the Tijuana Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel had formed a truce and that the Tijuana Cartel was seeking to merge with the Sinaloa Cartel.

descriptions: Possible truce or agreement between Tijuana and Sinaloa cartels for peace.
edges.evidence_quote: The relative peace in the city of Tijuana in 2010–2012 has raised speculations of a possible agreement between the Tijuana Cartel and the Sinaloa Cartel to maintain peace

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel is listed as a rival of Tren de Aragua in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: El infobox lista al Cartel de Sinaloa como enemigo del Tren de Aragua.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel de Sinaloa

descriptions: The United Nations lists the Sinaloa Cartel as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: United Nations allied with Sinaloa Cartel to exchange marijuana for cocaine.
edges.evidence_quote: Kaawach ... served as his liaison with the Sinaloa Cartel, working out deals to exchange the prized B.C. Bud for cocaine.

descriptions: Vineland Boys had ties to Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: revealing the Vineland Boys' ties to the Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: West End Gang member Maloney helped the Sinaloa Cartel smuggle cocaine into Ontario.
edges.evidence_quote: Maloney worked as a member of the Wolfpack, but transferred his loyalty towards helping the Sinaloa Cartel in smuggling cocaine direct into Ontario.

descriptions: The Sinaloa Cartel lost confidence in the Wolfpack and began operating independently in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sinaloa Cartel had long had doubts about the competence of the Wolfpack, especially Nero, and decided after his arrest to move into Canada themselves.

descriptions: The Wolfpack Alliance distributed cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack worked as distributors of cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel of Mexico, whose leader was Joaquín Guzmán.

descriptions: Wolfpack distributed cocaine supplied by the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack worked as distributors of cocaine from the Sinaloa Cartel of Mexico

descriptions: The Sinaloa Cartel used the Wolfpack as middlemen for drug distribution, then bypassed them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Wolfpack were used by Guzmán and the Sinaloa Cartel to establish a drug distribution network in Canada, which allowed them to ultimately by-pass the Wolfpack, as the Sinaloa cartel took over the networks that the Wolfpack had established.

descriptions: Yakuza is allied with Cartel de Sinaloa.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación , Los Viagras , Operativo Sierra Leona , Cartel de Sinaloa
